name: TN Quick Cash Hermitage OHB
description_short: Tennessee Quick Cash offers same-day title loans, flex lines of credit, and check cashing services across Middle Tennessee with instant funding up to $6,500.
description_long:
Tennessee Quick Cash is a regional financial services provider operating in Middle Tennessee with a focus on rapid cash access solutions. The company has positioned itself as an emergency lending option for consumers needing immediate funds, emphasizing speed of approval and funding in their marketing. The Hermitage location mentioned in this profile is now serviced through their Mt. Juliet branch at 12168 Lebanon Rd, suggesting some consolidation of physical locations.

The company offers three primary service lines: title loans that use vehicle titles as collateral with 30-day terms, Flex Loans which function as lines of credit with no setup fees or annual fees, and check cashing services with a 2% fee on payroll checks. They advertise the ability to provide up to $6,500 in same-day funding and claim applicants can qualify in minutes with a simple 5-minute online application. The Flex Loan product allows borrowers to draw on a credit line and pay interest only on the daily balance for the period funds are borrowed.

Tennessee Quick Cash distinguishes itself through the flexibility of Flex Loans, which explicitly state no penalty fees or annual charges, and the lack of prepayment penalties on title loans. They provide free vehicle title value estimates and offer practical financial services like check cashing at competitive rates and money order services. The company maintains online account access for Flex Loan customers and sends statements 14 days prior to due dates.

The major caveat is that title loans require full repayment or renewal every 30 days, and Tennessee state law mandates a 5% principal reduction fee on the third and subsequent renewals—a cost that compounds the overall loan expense. While advertised as emergency solutions, the short 30-day terms and renewal requirements create potential debt cycles. The company's emphasis on speed over affordability guidance and the limited transparency on APR rates for both products raise consumer protection concerns typical of the emergency lending sector.
pros: Flex Loans have no annual fees, setup fees, or penalty fees according to their website
Title loans have no prepayment penalties, allowing early payoff without additional charges
Same-day or instant funding advertised for qualified applicants
Free vehicle title value estimates provided in-store
Competitive check cashing rate of 2% on payroll checks
Online account access and email statements for Flex Loan customers
Fast application process advertised at 5 minutes
cons: Title loans require full repayment or renewal every 30 days, creating potential debt cycles
Tennessee law requires 5% principal reduction fees on third and subsequent title loan renewals, increasing total cost
No APR rates disclosed on website for either title loans or Flex Loans
Physical Hermitage location has been consolidated into Mt. Juliet location, reducing local accessibility
Limited information about credit requirements, maximum loan amounts for Flex Loans, or interest rate ranges
best_for: Consumers with vehicles needing short-term emergency cash within 30 days
People seeking check cashing services without banking relationships
Borrowers preferring flexible credit lines without annual fees over traditional payday loans
diagnosis: Best for consumers facing genuine emergencies who have vehicle equity and can repay within 30 days, or those needing check cashing services. Primary caveat: title loan 30-day renewal cycles and state-mandated fees on renewals create debt entrapment risk; lack of disclosed APRs prevents informed comparison with alternatives.
services: Title loans using vehicle as collateral with 30-day terms
Flex Loans (unsecured lines of credit with no annual or setup fees)
Online account access for Flex Loan customers
Same-day or instant funding for qualified applicants
